stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the premium version) yields a guaranteed return of $70K. Option B (the budget version) depends on market demand: with probability 42-64% (uncertain), it yields $111K; otherwise it yields $26K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in market demand affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $70K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $61.7K to $80.4K. Point estimate: $71.1K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 51.8%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 51.8%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
